title: Tangy Tender Canadian Udon Casserole
description: A heartwarming, dairy‑free baked udon dish with a tangy lime‑maple glaze, tender tofu, and fresh veggies, inspired by Canadian comfort cuisine.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the udon noodles according to package directions until al dente; drain and set aside.
In a small bowl whisk together soy sauce, lime juice, coconut milk, maple syrup, minced garlic, and grated ginger to make the tangy sauce.
Press and crumble the tofu into bite‑size cubes; toss in a drizzle of the sauce until coated, then set aside.
In a large skillet, sauté the mushrooms and carrots over medium heat until they start to soften, about 5 minutes; add the spinach and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes.
Combine the cooked noodles, sautéed veggies, tofu, and remaining sauce in a 9‑x‑13‑inch baking dish, tossing gently to coat everything evenly.
Sprinkle the chopped pecans over the top and bake in a preheated oven at 180 °C (350 °F) for 20 minutes, or until the casserole is heated through and the cheese‑like topping is golden.
Remove from oven, let rest 5 minutes, then serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh cilantro or lime wedges.
Enjoy the creamy, tangy, and tender flavors in each bite!
